briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2025 Annual Municipal Report presented to Council
briefs.summary: Council will consider the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and a shorter Highlights version. This report is a yearly summary of the city's finances, services, and achievements. It is not a new law or bylaw. The report is presented for Council's information and discussion. No decision on new rules or spending is being made at this time.
briefs.source_body:
7.1
2025 ANNUAL MUNICIPAL REPORT AND ANNUAL MUNICIPAL REPORT HIGHLIGHTS
Purpose: To present the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and 2025 Annual Municipal Report Highlights.

briefs.title: Burnaby to replace Development Procedures Bylaw, update fees effective July 1, 2026
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on repealing the current Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw (2022) and replacing it with a new Development Procedures Bylaw (2026), effective July 1, 2026. The proposal also includes amendments to the Burnaby Building Bylaw and the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, with the same effective date. If approved, these changes would update rules and fees for development applications, building permits, and related processes, implementing the new Zoning Bylaw 2026 and the Development Approvals Process (DAP) Program.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on May 12, 2026 — if you have a development or building application in progress, the new rules and fees take effect July 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: Council vote on new Development Procedures Bylaw and fee changes
New Development Procedures Bylaw and fee changes take effect

briefs.source_body:
7.2
PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT OF BURNABY DEVELOPMENT PROCEDURES BYLAW AND RELATED AMENDMENTS TO THE BURNABY BUILDING BYLAW AND BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW
Purpose:
To seek Council approval to repeal
Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw, 2022
and replace it with the proposed
Development Procedures Bylaw, 2026
, to amend the
Burnaby
Building Bylaw, 2016
and
Burnaby
Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

attachments.title: Proposed Repeal and Replacement of Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw and Rela

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to apply for UBCM award on development approvals
briefs.summary: Council will consider endorsing an application for the 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Awards. This is based on the final report for Phase 3 of the Development Approvals Process (DAP) program. The DAP program aims to speed up and improve how the city handles development applications. If endorsed, the city would apply for the award, but no new rules or fees are proposed at this time.
briefs.source_body:
7.3
DAP PROGRAM - PHASE 3 FINAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide an update on the activities and outcomes of the Development Approvals Process (DAP) program and seek endorsement to apply for the 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Awards.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on mobile device contract award
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of mobile devices, services, subscription plans, and other data services for the City of Burnaby. If approved, this contract would set the terms for purchasing phones, tablets, and related data plans for city staff. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been approved.
briefs.source_body:
7.4
CA - MOBILE DEVICE AND PLANS
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of mobile devices, services, subscription plans, and other data services.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for aeration and overseeding services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for aeration and overseeding services. If approved, this would authorize the city to hire a company to perform these lawn and turf care services on city-owned properties. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
briefs.source_body:
7.5
CA - AERATION AND OVERSEEDING SERVICES
Purpose:
T
o obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for aeration and overseeding services.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for Victory St. road upgrades from Patterson to Jubilee
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for road upgrades on Victory Street, between Patterson Avenue and Jubilee Avenue. If approved, this project would fund construction work to improve the road. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
briefs.source_body:
7.6
CA - (CB-3233) VICTORY ST. - PATTERSON TO JUBILEE ROAD UPGRADES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Victory St. – Patterson to Jubilee Road Upgrades project.

briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on sidewalk contract for five Burnaby streets
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for new sidewalk construction on Triumph, Carleton, Rosser, Gilmore, and Madison streets. This is a proposed decision — if approved, the contract would be awarded to a construction company to build the sidewalks. The project is part of the city's ongoing work to improve pedestrian safety and infrastructure. Council will consider this item at an upcoming meeting; it has not yet been approved.
briefs.source_body:
7.7
CA - (CA-3299) TRIUMPH, CARLETON, ROSSER, GILMORE, AND MADISON SIDEWALK PACKAGE
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Triumph, Carleton, Rosser, Gilmore, and Madison Sidewalk Package project.

briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for Burnaby Sidewalk Expansion Program
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change for the Sidewalk Expansion Program (EB-8022). This change would adjust the existing contract for building new sidewalks. If approved, it could affect how and when new sidewalks are built in your area. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body:
7.8
CC - (EB-8022) SIDEWALK EXPANSION PROGRAM
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) to the (EB-8022) Sidewalk Expansion Program.

briefs.title: Council to consider comment letters on Metro 2050 land-use amendments for Maple Ridge and Surrey
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider sending comment letters to the Metro Vancouver Regional District about two proposed changes to the regional growth strategy, Metro 2050. One change is proposed by the City of Maple Ridge for land near North 256 Street, and the other by the City of Surrey for the Hazelmere area. These amendments would allow different types of development in those areas. Council has not yet decided what to say in the letters — this is a chance for the city to give its official feedback before the regional district makes a final decision.
briefs.source_body:
7.9
COMMENT LETTERS ON PROPOSED METRO 2050 AMENDMENTS: CITY OF MAPLE RIDGE (NORTH 256 STREET) and SURREY (HAZELMERE)
Purpose:
To inform Council about two different site-specific Metro 2050 Amendments, one proposed by the City of Maple Ridge and the other by the City of Surrey, and to consider forwarding comment letters to the Metro Vancouver Regional District.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to review street width standards for new developments
briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a motion that would direct city staff to review Burnaby's street width standards for works and services. This means the city would look at how wide streets need to be when new buildings or subdivisions are built. The goal is to see if current rules make streets too wide or not wide enough. A decision has not been made yet — this is just the first step. If approved, staff would study the issue and report back with possible changes.
briefs.action_required: No action needed now — watch for the staff report and any future public consultation.
briefs.source_body:
8.1
COTW - PROPOSED WORKS AND SERVICES REQUIREMENTS
Purpose: To direct staff to review the City's street width standards.

briefs.title: Council to vote on list of consultant budget cuts and retained funding for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposal that would require staff to prepare two separate lists: one showing all consultant budget cuts for 2026, including which departments and functions are affected, and another showing which consultants would keep their funding. This is not a final decision yet — Council will consider the motion at an upcoming meeting. If approved, the lists would help the city decide where to reduce spending on outside consultants next year.
briefs.action_required: No action needed from business owners at this time — watch for the council decision to see if your sector's consultants are affected.
briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - 2026 CONSULTANT FUNDNG AND BUDGET REDUCTIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for staff to provide a comprehensive list of consultant budget cuts, including associated functions and departments, and a separate list of consultants retaining funding for 2026.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan update and direction
briefs.summary: Council will consider a draft update to the Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan. This proposed plan could change how trails on Burnaby Mountain are managed, including rules for trail use, maintenance, and access. The decision is not final yet — Council is being asked for direction on the draft plan at this meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — if approved, new trail rules could affect businesses that use or operate near the trails.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
briefs.source_body:
8.3
PRCC - DRAFT BURNABY MOUNTAIN TRAIL MANAGEMENT PLAN
Purpose: To provide an update and seek Council direction on the Draft Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan.

briefs.title: Council to consider supporting Vancouver Roques Rugby's Bingham Cup 2028 bid
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a request from the Vancouver Roques Rugby club to support their bid to host the Bingham Cup in 2028. The Bingham Cup is a major international rugby tournament. Council's support would mean the City officially backs the bid, which could help bring the event to the region. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
briefs.source_body:
8.4
PRCC - VANCOUVER ROQUES RUGBY - BINGHAM CUP 2028 SUPPORT REQUEST
Purpose: To seek Council support for Vancouver Roques Rugby's bid to host the Bingham Cup in 2028.

briefs.title: Proposed: Non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue. Non-market housing means homes that are rented at below-market rates, often run by a non-profit or government agency, to make them more affordable. Council will consider approving the project at an upcoming meeting. This is not a final decision yet — it is a proposal that still needs council approval.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments to have your say on this proposed housing project.
briefs.source_body:
9.1
PROPOSED NON-MARKET HOUSING PROJECT AT 3961 AND 3983 NORLAND AVENUE
Purpose: To provide an update on the proposed non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 to replace 1965 version
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed new zoning bylaw that would replace the current 1965 version. The proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 would repeal the old bylaw and introduce new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignments. This means property owners and businesses may see changes to what they can build, how they can use their land, and what permits they need. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider the proposal at upcoming meetings.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date and attend to voice your concerns or support for the proposed zoning changes.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14815 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update penalty rules for Zoning Bylaw 1965 violations
briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw that would update the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009. Specifically, it would remove the old penalty schedule for the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with a new schedule for the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026. This means if you own property or run a business in Burnaby, the fines and enforcement process for zoning violations could change once the new bylaw is approved. The decision has not been made yet — council is set to vote on this amendment at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, this could affect how zoning penalties are enforced for your property or business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14816 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (repeal Schedule A, Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 section and replace with Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to amend definitions of 'affordable housing' in ACC and DCC waiver bylaw
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the definitions of 'affordable housing' and 'Zoning Bylaw' in the Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of Amenity Cost Charges (ACC) and Development Cost Charges (DCC) Bylaw 2024. Amenity Cost Charges are fees developers pay to fund community amenities like parks and community centres. Development Cost Charges are fees for infrastructure like roads and sewers. The proposed changes could affect which housing projects qualify for fee waivers or reductions, potentially impacting development costs and housing affordability in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your development project's eligibility for fee waivers.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14817 - Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of Amenity Cost Charges and Development Cost Charges Bylaw 2024, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of ACC and DCC Bylaw 2024 (definitions of "affordable housing" and "Zoning Bylaw")

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026 for redevelopment impacts
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026. This bylaw would create new rules to protect tenants who are forced to move because their building is being redeveloped (torn down or renovated). The proposed rules could include things like requiring landlords to give tenants more notice, pay moving costs, or offer the right to return to a new unit at a similar rent. Council will consider this proposal at an upcoming meeting — it has not been approved yet.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — you may want to attend or submit comments if you are a landlord or tenant affected by redevelopment.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Construction & Development
briefs.source_body:
11.1.4
#14821 - Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish protections for tenants impacted by redevelopment

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment to strengthen tenant protection enforcement
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. This change would update how the city enforces rules that protect tenants. If approved, it could mean faster or stronger penalties for landlords who break tenant protection rules. The exact details of the new enforcement powers are not yet public, but the goal is to give the city better tools to hold landlords accountable.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how tenant complaints are handled against your property.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.5
#14822 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (tenant protection)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to vote on mid-year fee and charge updates for 2026
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the City of Burnaby's Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw with mid-year changes for 2026. This means fees for various city services, permits, and licences could go up or be adjusted. The decision has not been made yet — this is a proposal up for approval at a future council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and review the proposed fee changes — they could affect your business licence or permit costs.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.6
#14823 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (2026 mid-year amendments)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on e-bike and scooter pilot bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend the existing Street and Traffic Bylaw to allow a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, this pilot would set new rules for where and how these devices can be used on city streets and paths. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider the amendment at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new rules for e-bikes and scooters could affect your business operations or deliveries.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and scooter pilot in parks
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in certain Burnaby parks as part of a pilot program. The proposed amendment to the Parks Regulation Bylaw would create new rules for where and how these devices can be used. If approved, the pilot would let riders use e-bikes and scooters on designated park paths and trails, but not on all park areas. The decision has not been made yet — this is a proposal up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, check which park trails are open to e-bikes and scooters before riding.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw changes for e-bike and e-scooter pilot
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009. This proposed change is part of a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, it would update how the city enforces rules for these devices, including how tickets and fines are issued. The decision has not been made yet — council will vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, this could affect how e-bike and e-scooter rules are enforced in Burnaby.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 sets new property tax rates
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of the Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would set the tax rates for all taxable land and buildings (called 'improvements') in the city for the 2026 tax year. It is based on sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter, which give the city the authority to set these rates. If approved, this bylaw will determine how much property tax you pay next year.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the
Community Charter

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on abandoning rezoning for 3900 Grand Promenade
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would abandon a previous rezoning for 3900 Grand Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858/9898 Gatineau Place. The original rezoning, approved in September 2024, allowed two residential towers (80 and 73 storeys) plus a 13-storey retail and office podium. The applicant now wants to remove the office space and add 292 more market strata residential units instead. Council will consider whether to scrap the old plan and start fresh with this new proposal.
briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
briefs.source_body:
11.3.1
#14737 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2025 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858 and 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 and 73 storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. The applicant is proposing to remove commercial office use and replace it with additional market strata residential use, which includes the proposal of 292 additional market strata units
